extraction.ocr_text:
Select agent type

Autonomous
Create an agent that acts
independently to complete
a given task.

Conversational
Create an assistant that
interacts with users
through real-time dialogue.

Coded
Preview
Import a coded agent
project built in your IDE into
Studio.

Get started with Autopilot

What should your agent do?

The conversational agent that will use available tools from UiPath Orchestrator and context
grounding indexes to answer various questions about: Invoice processing documents, ServiceNow
incidents and associated Orchestrator Jobs.

Start fresh
Generate Agent
extraction.description: This screenshot shows a modal in UiPath Studio Web for creating a new agent and selecting the agent type. The Conversational option is selected, and the Autopilot prompt area is filled with a description of the agent’s intended behavior. The dialog also shows the available alternatives Autonomous and Coded, along with the Start fresh and Generate Agent actions.
extraction.step_instructions:
1. Select the Conversational agent type.
2. In the Autopilot description field under “What should your agent do?”, enter the agent use-case description.
3. Click on “Generate Agent” to let Autopilot create the agent configuration, or click on “Start fresh” if you want to configure it manually.
